analysis:
Three independent bugs in apps/api targeting the tickets domain.

**US-002 (Bug #20):** `tickets.service.ts` lines 73–75 guard against empty-string descriptions and throw `ValidationAppException`. However, line 94 already coerces `description || null`, so the guard is redundant and incorrectly blocks semantically valid empty-description payloads from agents. Fix: delete the three-line guard. No schema or DTO changes needed.

**US-003 (Bug #21):** `UpdateTicketDto` (`dto/update-ticket.dto.ts`) has no `status` field so PATCH `/tickets/:ref` silently drops any `status` sent by clients. `TicketsService.update()` never calls `validateTransition()` either. Fix: (1) add `status?: TicketStatus` with `@IsOptional()` + `@IsEnum()` to the DTO; (2) add a block in `update()` that calls `validateTransition(ticket.status, updateTicketDto.status)` then sets `updateData.status`. Only 'NONE'-comment transitions (CREATED→IN_PROGRESS, VERIFIED→IN_PROGRESS) can be triggered here — comment-gated transitions still belong to dedicated endpoints. `validateTransition` is already exported from `./state-machine/ticket-transitions`.

**US-004 (Bug #23):** `TicketResponseDto` (`dto/ticket-response.dto.ts`) has no `ref` field. `findAll()` in `tickets.service.ts` maps tickets without adding `ref`; `findByRef()` and `create()` already compute and return `ref`. Fix: (1) add `ref!: string` with `@ApiProperty` to `TicketResponseDto`; (2) add `ref: `${project.key}-${ticket.number}`` inside the `findAll()` items map — `project` is already in scope.

**Test infrastructure:** Unit tests live in `src/tickets/tickets.service.spec.ts` using Jest mocks of `PrismaService`. E2E tests live in `test/e2e/api-endpoint/endpoint.e2e.spec.ts` using supertest. The `mockProject` fixture already has `key: 'KODA'`. All three stories are independent and can be implemented in any order.

userStories.title: Remove empty-description guard in ticket create
userStories.description: TicketsService.create() throws ValidationAppException when description is an empty string, but this is semantically valid — the coercion on line 94 already converts empty string to null. Remove the three-line guard (lines 73–75 of tickets.service.ts) so agents can create tickets with description: ''.
acceptanceCriteria: When TicketsService.create() is called with createTicketDto.description === '', it returns a ticket object where description === null
When TicketsService.create() is called with createTicketDto.description === undefined, it returns a ticket where description === null
When TicketsService.create() is called with createTicketDto.description === 'some text', it returns a ticket where description === 'some text'
When POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets is called with body { type: 'BUG', title: 'T', description: '' }, it returns HTTP 201 with response body data.description === null

routing.reasoning: Single file change — delete 3 lines from tickets.service.ts. Unit tests and one E2E case cover all ACs. Well under 50 LOC.

userStories.title: Fix ticket status PATCH no-op
userStories.description: PATCH /tickets/:ref silently ignores any status field in the request body because UpdateTicketDto has no status property and TicketsService.update() never processes it. Add status?: TicketStatus to the DTO and add a validateTransition() call followed by updateData.status assignment inside update().
acceptanceCriteria: When TicketsService.update() is called with updateTicketDto.status === 'IN_PROGRESS' on a ticket with status === 'CREATED', it calls validateTransition('CREATED', 'IN_PROGRESS') without throwing and returns an updated ticket where status === 'IN_PROGRESS'
When TicketsService.update() is called with updateTicketDto.status === 'IN_PROGRESS' on a CREATED ticket, the Prisma db.ticket.update() call receives data containing status: 'IN_PROGRESS'
When TicketsService.update() is called with an invalid transition (e.g. status === 'CLOSED' on a CREATED ticket), validateTransition throws ValidationAppException and db.ticket.update() is not called
When TicketsService.update() is called with no status field in updateTicketDto, updateData does not contain a status key
When PATCH /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref is called with body { status: 'IN_PROGRESS' } on a CREATED ticket, it returns HTTP 200 with response body data.status === 'IN_PROGRESS'

routing.reasoning: Two files modified (DTO + service), introduces state-machine integration in a new code path, 4 distinct AC branches. 50–100 LOC total. Warrants three-session-tdd-lite for correct test isolation.

userStories.title: Add ref field to ticket API responses
userStories.description: GET /tickets and GET /tickets/:ref do not include a ref field (e.g. NAX-1) in responses because TicketResponseDto has no ref property and findAll() does not compute it. Add ref!: string to the DTO and add ref computation in findAll(). create() and findByRef() already return ref — add E2E/unit assertions for those too.
acceptanceCriteria: TicketResponseDto has a non-optional property ref of type string decorated with @ApiProperty({ description: 'Project-scoped ticket reference, e.g. NAX-1' })
When TicketsService.findAll() returns items, each item's ref equals '${project.key}-${ticket.number}' (e.g. 'KODA-1' for key 'KODA' and number 1)
When GET /api/projects/:slug/tickets is called, each item in the response data.items array contains a ref string matching the pattern /^[A-Z0-9]+-[1-9][0-9]*$/
When GET /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref is called for an existing ticket, the response data.ref equals '${projectKey}-${ticketNumber}'
When POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets is called to create the first ticket in a project with key 'KODA', the response data.ref equals 'KODA-1'

routing.reasoning: Additive change: one new field in DTO, one line added to findAll() map. findByRef() and create() already produce ref. Under 50 LOC total across 2 files.
